Japanese: ９月９日　午前９時５２分 地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: September 9, 9:52 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1

Japanese: ‥‥い、い、いよいよ、 なんですね。
English: Well... I guess this is it!

Japanese: うん。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: 今日で、この事件はケリがつくよ。
English: One way or another, this case gets decided today.

Japanese: あっ！ べ、弁護士さん、見て‥‥！
English: Aaa! Ph-Phoenix! Look!

Japanese: （御剣‥‥検事）
English: (Prosecutor... Edgeworth.)

Japanese: ‥‥昨日、検事局長に呼ばれた。
English: I received a call from the public prosecutor's office yesterday.

Japanese: 今日の証人・小中氏が語ることは、 “絶対”だと言われた。
English: He told me that whatever Mr. White says today, it will be the "absolute truth."

Japanese: キミが、彼の証言に対して どんな攻撃をしようと、
English: No matter how you try to attack his testimony...

Japanese: 私が異議をとなえれば、裁判長は かならず聞き入れるだろう、と。
English: If I raise an objection, I have it on good faith that the judge will listen to me.

Japanese: （裁判長にまで、小中のチカラは およんでいるのか‥‥？）
English: (What, does White have the judge in his pocket, too!?)

Japanese: ‥‥つまり、ぼくの有罪は もう決まってる、というワケだ。
English: So... you're saying I'm going to be guilty. End of story?

Japanese: 私は、有罪判決を勝ちとるため ならば、なんでもする男だ。
English: I will do anything to get my verdict, Mr. Wright. Anything.

Japanese: どうしてなんですか！
English: Why... Why!?

Japanese: あなたはそうやって、 罪のない人を苦しめて‥‥！
English: How can you torment an innocent person like this!?

Japanese: “罪のない”‥‥？
English: Innocent...?

Japanese: そんなことが、どうして 私たちにわかる？
English: How can we know that?

Japanese: 罰を逃れるためならば、彼らは どんなウソだってつく。
English: The guilty will always lie, to avoid being found out.

Japanese: 見わけることなんてできない。 ‥‥それならば。
English: There's no way to tell who is guilty and who is innocent!

Japanese: 私にできることは、１つ。 被告人を、すべて有罪にする。
English: All that I can hope to do is get every defendant declared "guilty"!

Japanese: それが私のルールだ。
English: So I make that my policy.

Japanese: 御剣‥‥。
English: Edgeworth...

Japanese: 変わったな、お前。
English: You've changed.

Japanese: え？
English: Hmm?

Japanese: 弁護士さん！ 知ってるの、この人のこと‥‥？
English: Phoenix! You know him!?

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一。 ‥‥手かげんはしないぞ。
English: Don't expect any special treatment, Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: べ、弁護士さん‥‥？
English: Ph-Phoenix...?

Japanese: さ、そろそろ開廷時間だね。
English: Well... court will be opening for session soon.

Japanese: ‥‥あ、で、でも！
English: What? But wait!

Japanese: 弁護士さんを弁護する 弁護士さんが、まだ‥‥！
English: Your defense attorney isn't even here yet! He's not...

Japanese: 弁護は、ぼくが自分でやる。
English: I'll be defending myself.

Japanese: えええっ！
English: Whaaaat!?

Japanese: さ。行くよ。
English: Okay, let's do this.

Japanese: ９月９日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第１法廷
English: September 9, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 1

Japanese: これより、成歩堂　龍一の 法廷を開廷します。
English: The court is now in session for the trial of Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: 検察側、 準備完了しております。
English: The prosecution is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: 弁護側、 準備完了しております。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。 ‥‥本当にいいんですね？
English: Mr. Wright... Are you sure you're up to doing this?

Japanese: はい。 自分の弁護は、自分でします。
English: Yes, Your Honor. I will be defending myself.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: Understood.

Japanese: では、御剣検事。 ‥‥冒頭弁論を。
English: Very well. Mr. Edgeworth, your opening statement, please.

Japanese: 事件の流れは、すでに あきらかであるから、
English: As the details of the event are already quite clear to the court,

Japanese: 本日は、被告人の犯行を 見た証人の証言を聞いていただく。
English: today we will hear the testimony of another witness to the defendant's crime.

Japanese: いいでしょう。
English: I see.

Japanese: ‥‥では、さっそく呼んで もらいましょう。
English: The prosecution may call its witness.

Japanese: （‥‥話がスムーズに 進みすぎる‥‥）
English: (That went far too smoothly!)

Japanese: （ふつうなら、なぜ小中が 前回の法廷に出てこなかったか）
English: (Why didn't the judge ask Edgeworth why his witness didn't testify before!?)

Japanese: （問題になるはずなのに、 そんなようすはない‥‥）
English: (It's like... it's like he already knows why!)

Japanese: （どうしよう‥‥。ムダだとは 思うけど、言ってみるか？）
English: (Hmm. If anyone's going to raise an objection about this, I suppose it's me...)

Japanese: 御剣検事に、お聞きしたい！
English: Mr. Edgeworth, you owe an explanation to the court!

Japanese: その証人はなぜ、綾里　真宵の 裁判には出廷しなかったのか！
English: Why didn't this witness testify in the trial against Ms. Maya Fey!?

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: すまなかったねえ。
English: I'm ever so sorry!

Japanese: 小中氏は多忙な人物なのだ。 そしてあのときは、
English: Mr. White is a busy man, and besides...

Japanese: 松竹　梅世の証言だけで じゅうぶんだと思ったものでねえ。
English: at the time, I thought that Ms. May's opinion was all that would be needed.

Japanese: もうしわけない。
English: Again, my sincerest apologies to the court.

Japanese: ‥‥さすが、御剣検事。 いさぎよいタイド、ですな。
English: Excellent, Mr. Edgeworth. I appreciate your demeanor.

Japanese: （くそっ。ヤツがカッコイイところ を見せただけで終わっちまった）
English: (Great. He gets to show off and I get nowhere.)

Japanese: （‥‥やめておこう。 ワナが待っているかもしれない）
English: (Forget it. I smell a trap.)

Japanese: では、小中　大（まさる）氏を 入廷させていただこう！
English: I would like to call Mr. Redd White to the stand!

Japanese: 証人の名前をうかがいたい。
English: Please state your full name.

Japanese: イエス・アイアム！
English: You wish to know the title of my personage?

Japanese: 名前を、聞いている！
English: Er... your name?

Japanese: おー！　ソーリー。
English: Yes! That is what I said!

Japanese: アメリカ生活が長いもんでね。 ニガテなんだよ、ニポンゴ‥‥。
English: Oh dear, do my locutions confuse?

Japanese: 名前だッ！
English: Name!

Japanese: （‥‥いいコンビだ）
English: (These two are great together...)

Japanese: ぼかぁ、小中　大。 マサル・コナーカ。
English: My name is Redd White. But my friends call me Blanco Nino.

Japanese: 株式会社《コナカルチャー》の 社長、いやプレジデントだ。
English: I am the CEO, or to use a more common term, the President, of Bluecorp.

Japanese: 被害者の綾里　千尋を ごぞんじか？
English: Did you know the victim, Ms. Mia Fey?

Japanese: ネヴァー・ギブアップ！ ‥‥知らないねえ。
English: That would be a negatory! No, I did not.

Japanese: 証人は、事件のあった夜 板東ホテルに‥‥？
English: You were at the Gatewater Hotel the night of the murder?

Japanese: イエス。
English: Correct.

Japanese: そこで、事件を目撃 したというわけだ。
English: And you witnessed the murder from there?

Japanese: アハン。 よくわかってるじゃないか。
English: Ahem. Why tell you what you already know?

Japanese: では、小中さん。‥‥《証言》を していただきましょう。
English: Very well, Mr. White. You may begin your testimony.

Japanese: （どうにかして、コイツの証言を くずさなきゃ、おしまいだ‥‥）
English: (If I can't rip this guy's testimony apart, I'm done for.)

Japanese: （まったく、どうしていつもこう、 ガケっぷちに立たされるんだ？）
English: (Why do I always feel like it's the end of the world and I'm the last man standing?)

Japanese: ふっふっふ‥‥。
English: Ho hoh hoh.

Japanese: カクゴはいいかい、 ミスタ・ベンゴシ‥‥。
English: I hope you have made your peace with God, Mr. Lawyer!

Japanese: 弁護士さん！　がんばって！
English: Let him have it, Phoenix!

Japanese: ～事件の当夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: あれはそう、ちょうど９時 ごろのことだったよ。
English: Let's see, it was about 9:00, I believe.

Japanese: ぼかぁ、窓のそばで書類を リーディング、読んでいたんだ。
English: I was quietly perusifying... er, that's "reading" to you, some papers by the window.

Japanese: すると、窓のそとで 物音がしたんだよ！
English: Then I heard a bedlam coming from outside!

Japanese: ぼかぁ、その音におどろいて、 向かいのビルを見たねえ。
English: Surprised, I turned to look at the building across the way.

Japanese: すると、ギザギザヘアの男が ロングヘアのレディを襲っていた！
English: It was then I saw him: a spiky-haired man attacking a woman with long hair!

Japanese: 言うまでもなく、キミだよ ミスタ・ベンゴシ！
English: Needless to say that man was none other than you, Mr. Lawyer!

Japanese: ぼかぁ、梅世クンを呼んだねえ！ 彼女も、それはビックリだよ！
English: I called Miss May over at once. She, too, was flabbergasted of course.

Japanese: 被害者は、その‥‥、必死に 逃げたが、キミは追いすがった！
English: The victim, she... she ran away, but you gave chase!

Japanese: ジャスト・フィット！ ‥‥それで、ジ・エンドさ。
English: Finally there was a terrible impaction! Then it was all over...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: その目撃に間違いがなければ、 被告人の有罪は決定ですね‥‥。
English: If things occurred as you testify, then I'm afraid the defendant is guilty.

Japanese: では、被告人‥‥‥、あ、いや、 弁護人。
English: Very well, defendant... er, I mean, Mr. Wright.
Your cross-examination...

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ～事件の当夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: あれはそう、ちょうど９時 ごろのことだったよ。
English: Let's see, it was about 9:00, I believe.

Japanese: ぼかぁ、窓のそばで書類を リーディング、読んでいたんだ。
English: I was quietly perusifying... er, that's "reading" to you, some papers by the window.

Japanese: すると、窓のそとで 物音がしたんだよ！
English: Then I heard a bedlam coming from outside!

Japanese: ぼかぁ、その音におどろいて、 向かいのビルを見たねえ。
English: Surprised, I turned to look at the building across the way.

Japanese: すると、ギザギザヘアの男が ロングヘアのレディを襲っていた！
English: It was then I saw him: a spiky-haired man attacking a woman with long hair!

Japanese: 言うまでもなく、キミだよ ミスタ・ベンゴシ！
English: Needless to say that man was none other than you, Mr. Lawyer!

Japanese: ぼかぁ、梅世クンを呼んだねえ！ 彼女も、それはビックリだよ！
English: I called Miss May over at once. She, too, was surprised of course.

Japanese: 被害者は、その‥‥、必死に 逃げたが、キミは追いすがった！
English: The victim, she... she ran away, but you gave chase!

Japanese: 左に向かって、被害者は逃げた。 キミはそれに追いすがった！
English: The victim ran to the left, and you gave chase!

Japanese: ジャスト・フィット！ ‥‥それでジ・エンドさ。
English: Finally there was a terrible impaction! Then it was all over...

Japanese: （‥‥こいつも御剣の手ほどきを 受けているのか‥‥？）
English: (Did Edgeworth "prep" this guy, too, I wonder?)

Japanese: （とにかく、なんとかして 突破口を見つけるしかない！）
English: (Anyway, I'd better find a weakness in there...)

Japanese: その時間は、 どうしてわかるんですか？
English: How do you know what time it was?

Japanese: なぜなら、ぼかぁ常に、 カンペキにパーフェクトだからさ。
English: Because I am always abso-posi-lutely perfect, you know?

Japanese: いやいやいや。 それじゃダメです。
English: No no no, you're not getting away with that!

Japanese: うたぐりぶかい男だね。
English: You are so mistrusting, Mr. Lawyer!

Japanese: うーん、‥‥秘書‥‥ 英語で何て言ったっけ‥‥？
English: So... what was the proper term for "secretary" again...?

Japanese: とにかく、梅世くんが９時に ルームサービスをたのんだんだ。
English: Anyway, Miss May ordered room service for 9:00.

Japanese: それが届いたときの ことだったからね。
English: It happened soon after the room service arrived.

Japanese: （梅世もそんなことを 言っていたけど‥‥）
English: (Hmm. That's what Miss May said, too.)

Japanese: たしかに、コーヒーを運んだ ボーイは、梅世さんを見ています。
English: True, the bellboy who brought the coffee saw Miss May.

Japanese: でも彼は、あなたは見ていないと 証言している！
English: But he testified that he did not see you at the time!

Japanese: アハン‥‥そのことかい。
English: Ahem. This is your concern?

Japanese: 梅世クンは、部屋のそとで コーヒーを受け取ったからねえ。
English: Silly Lawyer! Miss May received the coffee outside the room!

Japanese: 見えなくてトーゼンだねえ。
English: Of course he could not see me. He would need X-ray vision to pull off something like that!

Japanese: （うーん‥‥）
その時間、証人は何をしていたか？
English: Tell us, what were you doing at that time?

Japanese: （ここを攻撃しても、おそらく ムダだろう）
English: (I don't see anything to gain by attacking him on this point any further.)

Japanese: そのときあなたは、 何をしていましたか？
English: Okay, what were you doing at the time?

Japanese: 窓というのは、綾里法律事務所の 真正面にある‥‥？
English: By window, you mean the one directly across from the Fey & Co. Law Office?

Japanese: イエス！　ホテルの窓は アレしかないよ。
English: Correct! That is the only window, you see.

Japanese: そこで、書類を？
English: And there you were reading... papers?

Japanese: イエス！
English: Correct!

Japanese: ビジネスホテルは、ビジネスを する場所だよキミ！
English: The Gatewater is a business- man's hotel, and I am a busy man who had business to do!

Japanese: “物音”ですか‥‥？
English: A "bedlam"?

Japanese: たぶん、そのときに 襲いかかったんだろうねぇ。
English: It must have been when you attacked, I assume.

Japanese: なるほど‥‥そして？
English: We see. Continue.

Japanese: その音を聞くまでは、書類を 読んでいたわけですか？
English: So you were reading your papers until you heard that sound?

Japanese: まあね。ぼかぁ、ノゾキは シュミじゃないんだなあ。
English: But of course. I am no snoop, peeping out of windows at night.

Japanese: （よく言うよ‥‥）
English: (No snoop? Yeah, right! You've made a career out of snooping!)

Japanese: ギ、ギザギザヘア？
English: S-spiky-haired!?

Japanese: その証言は、松竹　梅世の 発言とムジュンしています！
English: What you just said directly conflicts with Miss May's testimony!

Japanese: 松竹　梅世は、犯人の体型を “少女”だったと言いました！
English: Miss May clearly stated that the assailant looked like a "girl"!

Japanese: ぼかぁ、目がいいのが ジマンでね！
English: I've always been proud of my eyesight, Mr. Lawyer.

Japanese: 視力はいくつですか？
English: Just what is your eyesight?

Japanese: 両目を合わせて２．７さ！
English: Counting both eyes... 40!

Japanese: ご、合計しないでください！
English: 40!? Don't add them together!

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、それならば、 よく見えるでしょうね。
English: I think the witness is trying to say his eyesight is good.

Japanese: （くそ‥‥裁判長はコイツの 味方か‥‥）
English: (Hey, whose side is the judge on, anyway?)

Japanese: それで、証人はどうしたか？
English: And what did you do then?

Japanese: そのとき、梅世さんは何を していましたか？
English: What was Miss May doing at that time?

Japanese: テレビドラマを見終わって、 泣いていたよ。
English: She had just finished watching a soap opera on the TV, and was weeping openly.

Japanese: あなたは、彼女が盗聴していた ことは知っていましたか？
English: Did you know she had been tapping the Fey office phone?

Japanese: それは、本件とは関係ないことだ。
English: Irrelevant! That has nothing to do with the case at hand.

Japanese: かまわないよ。‥‥答えよう。
English: I care not. I will answer the lawyer's bold inquiry.

Japanese: あれは、秘書が 勝手にやったことさ。
English: Miss May was acting alone when she tapped the phone of this Fey woman.

Japanese: ‥‥政治家みたいな 口のきき方ですね‥‥。
English: You'd make a good politician, Mr. White.

Japanese: ふふん。‥‥ぼかぁ、 “プレジデント”だからねえ。
English: Ho hoh! After all, I am El Presidente.

Japanese: それで、どうなりましたか？
English: Please continue.

Japanese: もう少し、 くわしく思い出せませんか？
English: Can you be a little more detailed about that?

Japanese: 大事なことですから。
English: I think it's worth knowing exactly what happened.

Japanese: アンダスタン、アンダスタン。 ‥‥了解したよ。
English: Of course! Comprende! I understand!

Japanese: 被害者は、キミの攻撃を受けて、 そう、左に逃げたんだ。
English: The victim was attacked, by you, and ran to the left.

Japanese: キミは、それを追いかけていって、 殴りつけた！
English: You gave chase, and struck her down!

Japanese: 間違いないだろうか？
English: Are you sure?

Japanese: ぼかぁ常に、カンペキに パーフェクトだからね！
English: As you know, I am always abso-posi-lutely perfect!

Japanese: では、そのように証言を 変えてもらいましょう。
English: Perhaps you could change your testimony to reflect this new detail.

Japanese: 間違いありませんか？
English: Are you sure about that?

Japanese: 何度も言いたかぁないがね。
English: How many times must I say it?

Japanese: ぼかぁ、カンペキに パーフェクトなんだよ！
English: I am abso-posi-lutely perfect! End of story!

Japanese: （こっちだって、何度も 聞きたかぁないよ‥‥）
English: (How many times must I hear that?)

Japanese: あれ‥‥。弁護士さん？
English: Psst! Phoenix!

Japanese: なんか、ちょっと 気になりません？　今の‥‥。
English: Doesn't something about that strike you as a little odd?

Japanese: あの‥‥“フィット”じゃなくて、 “ヒット”では‥‥？
English: Um... "impaction"? Don't you mean just "impact"?

Japanese: ナンセンス！ 大切なのは、フィーリングだよ！
English: Ridiculosity! I think you need to brush up on your language skills!

Japanese: （これにつっこむのは、 たしかにナンセンスだな‥‥）
English: (On second thought, perhaps I'd better leave the whole language thing alone...)

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: Wait right there!

Japanese: ‥‥小中さん。 墓穴を掘りましたね‥‥。
English: Mr. White, you've dug your own grave!

Japanese: な、なんのことだい？
English: What is this!?

Japanese: あなたは“左”に逃げたと 言いましたね。
English: You said the victim ran to the "left."

Japanese: しかし、それは梅世さんの 証言とムジュンします。
English: But that directly contradicts Miss May's testimony!

Japanese: 彼女は、ハッキリ“右”へ 逃げたと証言しています！
English: She clearly stated that the victim ran "right"!

Japanese: あはん。たぶん‥‥、 彼女の見間違いじゃないかな？
English: Oh hoh hoh. It is simple. You have misheard her.

Japanese: それはどうでしょうか。
English: I think not.

Japanese: ‥‥上面図を見てください。
English: Look at the floor plans...

Japanese: 犯人は、この位置にいました。
English: The killer was here.

Japanese: そして被害者は、このあたり。
English: And the victim, here.

Japanese: あなたの言うとおり、 被害者が左に逃げたとしたら‥‥
English: If the victim ran to the left, as you claim she did...

Japanese: そっちは、出口のドアとは 逆方向になります。
English: She would have been running directly away from the door!

Japanese: 彼女は、逃げ道がない方へ 逃げたことになる‥‥
English: She would have been running into a dead end!

Japanese: かなり不自然な状況です！
English: Don't you find that odd?

Japanese: ヘ、ヘンだなあ‥‥。
English: Very strange...

Japanese: たしかに左に逃げたのを 見たんだよ、ぼかぁ‥‥。
English: I did see her run to the left... I did.

Japanese: 弁護士さん‥‥。あのカオ、 ウソはついてないみたい‥‥。
English: Phoenix... look at his face. I don't think he's lying about this one.

Japanese: たしかにね。
English: True.

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら、小中は 被害者が左に逃げるのを、）
English: (Maybe he really did see the victim run left...?)

Japanese: （実際に、目撃したみたいだな）
English: (So he did witness the killing...?)
(Wait a second!)

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: 松竹　梅世は“右”と言い、 小中さんは“左”と言っている。
English: Miss May says "right," and Mr. White says "left."

Japanese: このムジュンについて、 あなたはどう考えますか？
English: Can you explain this contradiction to the court?

Japanese: 小中さんの証言どおり、千尋さんは 左に逃げたと思います。
English: As Mr. White has testified, Mia ran to the left.

Japanese: しかし‥‥、 もしそうだとしたら、
English: But... But if that's the case...

Japanese: 被害者はわざわざドアとは逆の方へ 逃げたことになりますよ？
English: Why would she run in the opposite direction from the door?

Japanese: いいえ。当然、被害者はドアに 向かって逃げたはずです。
English: She did not. She was running towards the door, Your Honor.

Japanese: ハッ！ それではムジュンしてしまう！
English: Bwah! Now you're the one making a contradiction, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥つまり。
English: Not at all.

Japanese: 梅世さんの証言どおり、千尋さんは 右に逃げたんだと思います。
English: As Miss May has stated, Mia ran to the right after she was attacked.

Japanese: なんだあ？ ぼかぁウソなんかついていないよ！
English: What is this? I am not fashioning fabrications, I assure you!

Japanese: あのレディは、たしかに左に 逃げた！　ぼかぁ、見たんだ！
English: That lady ran to the left! I am sure of it!

Japanese: だとすれば、答えはただ１つです。
English: Then there can be only one explanation.

Japanese: ほう‥‥それは‥‥？
English: Eh...? And that is?

Japanese: ２人とも、本当のことを 言っていると思います。
English: Both witnesses are telling the truth. (For once...)

Japanese: ハッ！　お話にならない！
English: Hah! I doubt it!

Japanese: それでは、ムジュンは解決 しないではないか！
English: Er, rather, that does not clear up the contradiction!

Japanese: ‥‥２つの証言がムジュンしない 答えが、たった１つだけあります。
English: There is one scenario that would explain their conflicting accounts.

Japanese: な、なんだと‥‥！
English: What!?

Japanese: 小中さんが事件を目撃した場所は、 ホテルじゃなかったんですよ。
English: Obviously, the witness was not viewing the crime from the hotel!

Japanese: どういうことですか！ 弁護人！
English: Mr. Wright! What do you mean?

Japanese: 証人が事件を目撃した場所は、 ホテルではなかった、だと！
English: Yes, what do you mean, he was not "viewing the crime from the hotel"!?

Japanese: じゃあ、どこだったと言うんだ！
English: If he was not in the hotel, where could he have been!?

Japanese: 綾里法律事務所ですよ、モチロン。
English: In the law offices of Fey & Co., of course!

Japanese: もっと正確に言うならば、 このポイントです！
English: More specifically, he was standing here!

Japanese: （事件があったとき、小中が いたポイントを示そう！）
English: (Show the court where Mr. White was standing!)

Japanese: それは、ここです！
English: That's where he was!

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: Hold it right there, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 事件があったとき、 その場所にいたとしたら、
English: If he was there during the very act of murder...

Japanese: それは“共犯者”ということに なりますよ！
English: That would make him an accomplice!

Japanese: バカな！
English: Impossible!

Japanese: 事件があったとき、犯人の他に 共犯者が現場にいたなどという、
English: There was no one other than the criminal and victim in the room!

Japanese: そんな証言・証拠など、どこにも ないではないか！
English: There is no evidence, no proof to the contrary! None!

Japanese: す、すみません！ もう一度おねがいします。
English: S-sorry! Er... let me try that again!

Japanese: それは、ここです！
English: That's where he was!

Japanese: べ、弁護人！ 我々をバカにするつもりか！
English: M-Mr. Wright! We are not fools!

Japanese: そこは、“被害者”が 立っていたポイントではないか！
English: How could he have been exactly where the victim was standing!?

Japanese: （あ、し、しまった！）
English: (Oh... oh, right!)

Japanese: 弁護人！ ‥‥いいかげんにしなさい！
English: Mr. Wright! What is the meaning of this!?

Japanese: す、すみません！ もう一度おねがいします。
English: Sorry, Your Honor! Sorry! Let me try that again...

Japanese: それは、ここです！
English: This is where he was!

Japanese: ‥‥いいですか！
English: Look!

Japanese: 被害者がドアに向かって このように逃げたとき、
English: When the victim ran for the door...

Japanese: このポイントから見れば、“左”に 逃げたように見えるのです。
English: If he was watching from this point, to him it would appear that she ran to the "left."

Japanese: いいかげんにしたまえ！ そ、そのポイントは‥‥、
English: Please! This is no time for jokes in ill taste!

Japanese: “犯人”が立っていた ポイントではないか！
English: That is where the killer was standing!

Japanese: せ、静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! I will have order!

Japanese: 法廷の秩序を乱す者は、 退廷を命じます！
English: Anyone disturbing the order of this courtroom will be held in contempt!

Japanese: 成歩堂くん！ ‥‥なんということを！
English: Mr. Wright! What are you suggesting!?
R-r-rapscallion!!!

Japanese: 弁護人の推理は、コジツケに すぎないッ！
English: The postulations of the defense are a distortion of the truth, Your Honor!

Japanese: そうですね‥‥ かなり、ゴウインな感じが‥‥。
English: Indeed. They do seem a bit far-fetched...

Japanese: ふっふっふっ。
English: Ho hoh hoh!

Japanese: 楽しませてくれるねえ、 ミスタ・ベンゴシ。
English: You provide us with so much entertainment, Mr. Lawyer!

Japanese: （なんだ？ ‥‥笑っている？）
English: (What now!? He's... laughing!?)

Japanese: リメンバーリメンバー。 ‥‥今のシゲキで、思い出したよ。
English: The hilarity of the moment made me remember something...

Japanese: ちょっと、ゴカイをまねく言い方を してしまったみたいだねえ。
English: It appears I have been unclear, and for this, I apologize.

Japanese: ミスタ・サイバンチョ。
English: Mr. Your Honor...

Japanese: もう一度、《証言》させて もらえないかな。
English: Might I be allowed to testify once more?

Japanese: わかりました。 では《証言》をおねがいします。
English: Very well, let's hear your revised testimony.

Japanese: （ふん‥‥。一度くずれた 証言は、もう立て直せないさ）
English: (Good luck. You can't fix a broken testimony, buddy!)

Japanese: ～“左に逃げた”という証言～
English: -- She Ran to the "Left" --

Japanese: 梅世クンの証言は、正しいよ。 ‥‥モチロン、ぼくのも、ね。
English: Miss May's testimony was correct... as was mine!

Japanese: キミがレディに襲いかかったとき、 彼女はまず、左に逃げたんだ。
English: When you assaulted the girl, she first ran to the left.

Japanese: そして思いきり、彼女を殴った。 ‥‥これが、ぼくが見た部分だ。
English: And then you hit her, savagely! That is what I saw.

Japanese: 次に彼女は、最後の力を ふりしぼって、右に逃げたんだ。
English: Next, with the last of her strength, she ran to the right.

Japanese: キミはそれを追いかけて、 彼女にトドメをさした。
English: You chased her, and delivered the final blow.

Japanese: ‥‥ここが、 梅世クンが見た部分だ。
English: That is what Miss May saw.

Japanese: どうだい？ キミは２回、殴ったワケさ。
English: You see? You hit her twice!

Japanese: 思い出したかい？ ミスタ・ベンゴシ‥‥！
English: Don't you remember, Mr. Lawyer?

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 それなら、スジはとおりますね。
English: Hmm... That does seem to make sense.

Japanese: 弁護人。《尋問》は‥‥？
English: Will you be cross-examining the witness's testimony?

Japanese: しますよ、そりゃあ！
English: You bet I will! I mean, yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ～“左に逃げた”という証言～
English: -- She Ran to the "Left" --

Japanese: 梅世クンの証言は、正しいよ。 ‥‥モチロン、ぼくのも、ね。
English: Miss May's testimony was correct... as was mine!

Japanese: キミがレディに襲いかかったとき、 彼女はまず、左に逃げたんだ。
English: When you assaulted the girl, she first ran to the left.

Japanese: そして思いきり、彼女を殴った。 ‥‥これが、ぼくが見た部分だ。
English: And then you hit her, savagely! That is what I saw.

Japanese: 次に彼女は、最後の力を ふりしぼって、右に逃げたんだ。
English: Next, with the last of her strength, she ran to the right.

Japanese: キミはそれを追いかけて、 彼女にトドメをさした。
English: You chased her, and delivered the final blow.

Japanese: ‥‥ここが、 梅世クンが見た部分だ。
English: That is what Miss May saw.

Japanese: どうだい？ キミは２回、殴ったワケさ。
English: You see? You hit her twice!

Japanese: どう‥‥弁護士さん？
English: What do you think Phoenix?

Japanese: 思ったとおり、ボロを 出したよ‥‥！
English: I think we've got him right where we want him! He slipped!

Japanese: ヤツが笑ってられるのも、 今のうちだけさ。
English: Let him laugh for now. I'll soon wipe that smile off his face!

Japanese: それはどういう‥‥！
English: What do you mean by that!

Japanese: これから、それを トークするんじゃないか。
English: That is what I am about to explain!

Japanese: ニッポン人はすぐ、ケツロンを いそぐ‥‥悪いクセだよ。
English: You Americans, always so impatient! It does not become you.

Japanese: （あんたもニッポン人だろうが！）
English: (Hey buddy, you're an American, too!)

Japanese: “まず”ってどういうことですか？
English: What do you mean "first"?

Japanese: “まず”っていうのは、 “次に”の前だよ。
English: First? That is what comes before what happens "next." You do speak English, right?

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、おちついて聞きたまえ、 ミスタ・ベンゴシ。
English: Please, sit back, relax. I will try to use simple words for your benefit.

Japanese: とにかく彼女は、左に逃げた。 そして‥‥、
English: First, she ran to the left, and then...

Japanese: ぼくは殴ってない！
English: I didn't hit anyone!

Japanese: まあまあ。 ‥‥かくしなさんな。
English: Now, now, Mr. Wright. There is no point hiding things from this court.

Japanese: いやいやいや！
English: I'm not hiding anything!

Japanese: 弁護人。証言のジャマは やめてもらいたい！
English: The prosecution requests that the defense refrain from interrupting the testimony!

Japanese: やめてもらえますかな？
English: Mr. Wright, you have been warned.

Japanese: （なんでぼくが 怒られるんだよ‥‥）
English: (Why's he mad at me!?)

Japanese: “次に”ってなんですか！
English: What do you mean "next"!?

Japanese: “次に”っていうのは、 “まず”の次だよ。
English: Next is what comes after "first."

Japanese: そんなことはわかってます！
English: I know that!

Japanese: それだけわかっていれば ＯＫだよ、ミスタ・ベンゴシ。
English: Then that is all you need to know, Mr. Lawyer.

Japanese: とにかく、彼女は向きを変えて、 必死で右に逃げだしたんだ。
English: As I said, she turned and made a desperate dash to the right.

Japanese: その“キミは”っていうの、 やめてください！
English: Stop saying it was me!

Japanese: だって、 あれはキミだったからねえ。
English: But, it was you.

Japanese: 弁護人！　キミではないと 言うなら、証拠を見せるんだな！
English: Mr. Wright! If you claim that it was NOT you, then show us proof!

Japanese: （くそー、ぼくのイメージが どんどん悪くなるぞ‥‥）
English: (Argh! Can I do nothing right? Nothing!?)

Japanese: つづけていいかな。
English: May I continue?

Japanese: ２人は、別の部分を目撃していた、 というわけですか？
English: So each of you saw different parts...?

Japanese: デァツライ！ そのとおりだよ！
English: Abso-posi-lutely! That's right, of course!

Japanese: （“デァツライ”って、 学校じゃ習わなかったよな‥‥）
English: (Where did he pick up that annoying phrase...?)

Japanese: エニウェイ、とにかく！
English: Anyway, moving along!

Japanese: あなたは、２回とも 見ていたんですか？
English: So were you watching both times?

Japanese: まあね。
English: I suppose I was.

Japanese: 最初からそう証言しなかったのは ソーリーソーリー、ワルかった！
English: And please excuse me for not testifying to this sooner!

Japanese: ‥‥いいですか。
English: Mr. White!

Japanese: 被害者は、“１回だけ” 殴られて、死んでいるのです！
English: The victim died from a "single" blow!

Japanese: ‥‥何か、言うことは！
English: What do you have to say to that?
Er... Erp!

Japanese: （今がチャンスだ！ たたみこむぞ‥‥！）
English: (Now's my chance to hit him where it counts!)

Japanese: 小中さん。あなた、“カンペキに パーフェクト”だったのでは？
English: Mr. White. Wasn't it you who told this court you were abso-posi-lutely perfect?

Japanese: うう。
English: Mmph.

Japanese: ‥‥そのフレーズは、もう 使わないことにするよ。
English: I will refrain from using this phrase from now on.

Japanese: 裁判長。小中さんに、もう一度 《証言》をおねがいします。
English: Your Honor, if you could ask the witness for a new testimony...

Japanese: 証人の記憶は、コンランしている。
English: The witness is obviously confused, Your Honor!

Japanese: ここで、１０分の休憩を 申し入れたい！
English: I would like to request a 10 minute break!

Japanese: そ、そうですね‥‥
English: Yes... yes, quite.

Japanese: 記憶がコンランしているのは、 証人がウソをついているからです！
English: The witness is confused because he's lying!

Japanese: 今ここで休憩を入れるのは、 断固、反対します！
English: I emphatically request that there be no break, Your Honor!

Japanese: （‥‥そうだ！　早く続きを‥‥）
English: (Yeah! We want justice...!)

Japanese: （逃げるな！　小中ァ！）
English: (Don't let him get away!)

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: 《証言》をつづけましょう‥‥。
English: If the witness would care to revise his testimony...

Japanese: （傍聴人が味方についたぞ。 これでもう、逃げられない！）
English: (The crowd's on my side! No slipping out of this now, White!)

Japanese: いいですか？　小中さん？
English: Mr. White?

Japanese: イ、イエス‥‥。
English: O... okay...

Japanese: ～２人の証言の食い違い～
English: -- The Two Accounts --

Japanese: そのぉ、なんだ。あれが倒れる音で ぼかぁ向かいの窓を見たわけだが。
English: Umm, well, see--I looked at the other window when I heard that thing fall.

Japanese: 次の瞬間、ミス・チヒロは左に 逃げていたんだよ！
English: Then, the next moment, I saw Miss Mia run to the left!

Japanese: 犯人‥‥キミは、襲いかかったが、 彼女はそれを‥‥かわしたんだ。
English: The killer, you, attacked her... but she dodged.

Japanese: ‥‥そして、そのぅ、
English: Umm... and then...

Japanese: 向きを変えて、 ドアの方へ逃げようとした！
English: She turned, and ran for the door!

Japanese: キミは、そこで１発、ポカリと やったんだ！
English: Then you did her in with a single blow! Thwap!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ポカリとねえ‥‥。
English: Hmm. "Thwap" indeed.

Japanese: では‥‥《尋問》を。
English: Very well, you may begin the cross-examination.

Japanese: ミ、ミスタ・サイバンチョ。
English: Mr. Your Honor...

Japanese: ぼかぁちょっと、おなかが イタいんだが‥‥。
English: My stomach, you see, it is hurting...

Japanese: ガマンしてください。
English: Deal with it.

Japanese: もうちょっとで 終わりますからね‥‥。
English: This is almost over.

Japanese: ～２人の証言の食い違い～
English: -- The Two Accounts --

Japanese: そのぉ、なんだ。あれが倒れる音で ぼかぁ向かいの窓を見たわけだが。
English: Umm, well, see--I looked at the other window when I heard that thing fall.

Japanese: 向かいの窓を見たら、電気スタンド が転がっていたんだよ。
English: A light stand was lying on the floor when I looked.

Japanese: 次の瞬間、ミス・チヒロは左に 逃げていたんだよ！
English: Then, the next moment, I saw Miss Mia run to the left!

Japanese: 犯人‥‥キミは、襲いかかったが、 彼女はそれを‥‥かわしたんだ。
English: The killer, you, attacked her... but she dodged.

Japanese: そして、向きを変えて、ドアの方へ 逃げようとした！
English: She turned, and ran for the door!

Japanese: キミは、そこで１発、ポカリと やったんだ！
English: Then you did her in with a single blow! Thwap!

Japanese: 弁護士さん。
English: Phoenix!

Japanese: ‥‥だんだん、 口数が減ってきましたね。
English: He's getting a lot less talkative.

Japanese: つっこまれるのを 恐れているんだろうね。
English: He's probably afraid I'll catch him on something!

Japanese: （‥‥やりにくいな‥‥）
English: (But it won't be easy!)

Japanese: “あれが倒れる音”‥‥。 “あれ”ってなんですか！
English: You heard "that thing fall"? What exactly was "that thing"?

Japanese: ん？　あ、あれ、“あれ”？ ‥‥ガラスの電気スタンドだよ！
English: Huh? Oh... oh, that? Umm... the glass light stand!

Japanese: （‥‥ああ。現場に倒れていた ヤツか‥‥）
English: (Right... the one that had fallen over at the scene.)

Japanese: 弁護士さん。
English: Phoenix!

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、ちょっと おかしくないですか？
English: Doesn't something about that strike you as odd?

Japanese: そうかな。 べつに、問題はないと思うよ。
English: Huh? No, not really.

Japanese: では、《尋問》を つづけてください。
English: Please continue your cross-examination.

Japanese: （たしかに、気になるな‥‥ もう少し、ゆさぶってみるか）
English: (Yeah, that is odd. I'll press further.)

Japanese: 小中さん。
English: Mr. White.

Japanese: な、なんだい？
English: Huh? W-what?

Japanese: あなたは、ガラスの電気スタンドを 見たわけですか？
English: You're saying you saw the glass light stand?

Japanese: そ、そうさ。
English: Y-yes.

Japanese: では、そのあたりを、証言 しなおしてください！
English: Then change your testimony to reflect that!

Japanese: ソ、ソーリーソーリー。 ‥‥すまないね。
English: S-sorry. My bad...

Japanese: では、小中さん。証言を 訂正してください。
English: The witness will revise his testimony.

Japanese: オ、オーケーオーケー。
English: Okay okay. Of course.

Japanese: “ミス・チヒロ”‥‥？
English: Miss Mia...?

Japanese: 小中さん。被害者のことを 知っていたんですか？
English: Mr. White, did you know the victim personally?

Japanese: の、の、の、のーだよ！ 知ってるハズがない！
English: No no no, triple no! How would I? How could I!

Japanese: 証人が被害者の名前を 知っていても、おかしくない！
English: I see nothing odd about the witness knowing the name of the victim.

Japanese: 弁護人！　証人を おどかさないでくれたまえ！
English: Mr. Wright! I will not have you harrying the witness!

Japanese: （御剣が、おちつきを なくしてきたな‥‥）
English: (You're looking a little flustered, Edgeworth!)

Japanese: さっきは、そこで被害者は 殴られた、と言ってましたよ？
English: Now before, you said that she was hit before she ran.

Japanese: カ、カン違いってヤツだ。
English: Y-yes, I must have been confused.

Japanese: ドンマイドンマイ！ 気にしないでくれたまえ。
English: But don't worry! We all know what happened now.

Japanese: そのとき、 あなたはどうしましたか？
English: What did you do then?

Japanese: もちろん、あわてて彼女を 追いかけたさ！
English: I gave chase, of course!

Japanese: のー！　ぼくじゃない！ は、ハンニンがだ！
English: No! No! Not me. Th-the killer, I mean! He gave chase!

Japanese: 証人。 あわてることはない。
English: Mr. White, please try to remain calm.

Japanese: 弁護人のハッタリには、 なんの証拠もないのだ！
English: Mr. Wright here is only trying to fluster you with baseless accusations!

Japanese: サ、サ、サンキュ、 ミスタ・ケンジ‥‥。
English: Oh... oh right. Thank you, Miles.

Japanese: “ポカリ”‥‥ですか？
English: Thwap...?

Japanese: 最初の証言から、ずいぶん おとなしい表現になりましたね？
English: That doesn't sound like the savage attack from your earlier testimony...

Japanese: は、ははっ！　ぼかぁ、 おとなしいオトコだからね。
English: Ho! Hoh hoh! Yes, well, I am a man of peace, you see.

Japanese: “電気スタンドが転がっていた”？ ‥‥初耳ですね。
English: The "glass stand was lying on the floor"? That's the first I've heard of this.

Japanese: なぜ、さっきはスタンドのことを 話してくれなかったんですか？
English: Why didn't you tell us about the stand before?

Japanese: な‥‥なぜだったかな‥‥？
English: W-why? Me?

Japanese: 誰かに言われたから‥‥いや！
English: W-well... I was instructed not to... wait!

Japanese: ジャスタ・モメン！ ‥‥ちょっと待ってくれたまえ。
English: One moment! Give me a minute to gather my thoughts.

Japanese: アタマがコンランしてきた‥‥。
English: I am so... so confused.

Japanese: （小中のヤツ、もうヘロヘロだぞ）
English: (White's falling apart.)

Japanese: 裁判長！　これ以上、証人を 苦しめないようにしてもらいたい！
English: Your Honor! Please, I ask that you do not allow the witness to be badgered any further!

Japanese: べ、弁護人。
English: M-Mr. Wright.

Japanese: むやみに証人を おどしつけないように。
English: Please refrain from badgering the witness...

Japanese: ‥‥はい。 （御剣も、必死だな‥‥）
English: Yes, Your Honor. (Edgeworth is on the ropes!)

Japanese: では、証人。つづきを。
English: Very well. The witness may continue his account.

Japanese: 小中さん。
English: Mr. White.

Japanese: 電気スタンドが転がっているのを 見るのは、不可能なんですよ！
English: It was impossible for you to have seen the glass stand!

Japanese: な、な、なんだと‥‥！
English: Whaaaat!?

Japanese: 小中さん。
English: Mr. White.

Japanese: 電気スタンドが転がっているのを 見るのは、不可能なんですよ！
English: It was impossible for you to have seen the light stand!

Japanese: な、な、なんだと‥‥！
English: Whaaaat!?

Japanese: スタンドは、コナゴナに 砕けていたんですよ！
English: The stand broke into pieces when it fell!

Japanese: うげっ！
English: Ooof!

Japanese: 砕けた破片だけを見ても、それが 電気スタンドだとはわからない！
English: Just by seeing the broken pieces you would have no idea it was a light stand!

Japanese: じゃあ、いったいあなたはいつ、 スタンドを見ることができたのか！
English: So tell me, exactly when was it that you saw the stand?

Japanese: 答えてください！

Japanese: き、決まってるだろう？ ‥‥た、倒れる前に見たのさ。
English: I-isn't it obvious? I saw the stand before it fell over!

Japanese: つまり、被害者が襲われる前に 見たワケですか？
English: So... you saw the stand before the victim was attacked, then?

Japanese: イ、イエス！ ‥‥それなら、問題あるまい？
English: C-correct! That would be no problemo, right?

Japanese: （どうだろう‥‥？）
‥‥そうですね。 それなら、問題ないと思います。
English: Right. I guess there isn't a problem.

Japanese: わかりました。 では‥‥、
English: Very well. Now...

Japanese: べ、弁護士さん！ ちょっと、待ってください！
English: Ph-Phoenix! Wait!

Japanese: え、何？

Japanese: “事務所の窓ごしに、 倒れる前のスタンドを見た”って、
English: He saw the stand through the office window before it fell over...

Japanese: あの人は そう言ってるんですよね？
English: That's what he's saying, right?

Japanese: ‥‥そうだね。

Japanese: そんなの、ムリじゃないですか！

Japanese: えっ‥‥な、なんで？

Japanese: だって、見えるはずないですよ！ 証拠もあります。
English: There's no way he could have seen it! And you have proof!

Japanese: あっ！

Japanese: どうしましたか？　成歩堂くん？

Japanese: すみません！ ‥‥やっぱり、問題が。
English: Sorry, Your Honor! I guess there is a problemo, er, I mean, a problem.

Japanese: ‥‥ふう。発言するときは、 よく考えなさい。
English: I see. Please try to think before you speak, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: すみません‥‥。
English: Sorry, Your Honor.

Japanese: でも、証拠もありますから。
English: But, I have proof!

Japanese: 大きな問題があります！
English: There's a big problemo, er, I mean problem here.

Japanese: な、なんだよ問題って‥‥？
English: What problem is this?

Japanese: 小中さん。あなたの言いぶんは、 こうですね？
English: Mr. White, let me make sure I have this straight...

Japanese: “殺人事件が起こるより前に、”
English: You saw the glass light stand through the window, from the hotel...

Japanese: “ホテルから、窓ごしに ガラスの電気スタンドを見た”
English: before the incident occurred?

Japanese: イエス！　そ、そうだよ！
English: Correct! That is so! It's conclusive, definitive, undeniable... unimpeachable!

Japanese: そんなことは、あり得ない！ スタンドを見るのは不可能です！
English: No, it's impossible! You couldn't have seen the stand!

Japanese: な、なぜですか！ ‥‥証拠でもあるのですかな？
English: What? Why couldn't he? You have proof?

Japanese: もちろんあります！
English: I sure do, Your Honor!

Japanese: ホテルにいる人間に、倒れる前の スタンドは、見ることができない！
English: A person in the hotel could not have seen the stand before it fell over!

Japanese: その証拠は、これ‥‥！
English: This is the proof!

Japanese: どうしました？
English: Something the matter?

Japanese: い、いや、その‥‥。
English: N-no... it's just...

Japanese: 弁護士さん、それ、 違うと思いますケド‥‥
English: Phoenix... I don't think that's what you meant.

Japanese: （ぼくもそう思う‥‥）
English: (I don't think so either.)

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。 いいかげんにするように。
English: Mr. Wright. This courtroom is no place to play practical jokes.

Japanese: ‥‥すみません。 もう一度、おねがいします。
English: I'm sorry, Your Honor. Let me try that again.

Japanese: これを見てください！
English: Look at this!

Japanese: それは‥‥現場の上面図、ですか？
English: These are the floor plans to the scene of the murder, yes?

Japanese: そのとおりです。 ‥‥いいですか。
English: Correct, Your Honor. Now, look...

Japanese: 現場の窓ごしに事務所を のぞくとなると‥‥、
English: If you were to look through the window at the office...

Japanese: 見えるエリアは、ズバリ！
English: This is the area you would be able to see!

Japanese: こうなります！
English: Here!

Japanese: ‥‥どうですか？
English: Well...?

Japanese: スタンドは、見えるエリアに 入っていません！
English: Note that the stand is not within the visible area!

Japanese: さあ！　小中さん！
English: Well, Mr. White!

Japanese: どうですかッ！
English: What do you have to say to that!?
Er... erp!
Ri... dicu... losity...

Japanese: いいですか、小中さん。
English: Mr. White.

Japanese: あなたがもし、板東ホテルに いたのならば。
English: If you were in the Gatewater hotel as you claim,

Japanese: 倒れる前のスタンドは、 見ることはできなかった。
English: you could not have seen the stand before it fell over!

Japanese: そして、倒れた後も、 見ることはできなかった！
English: In fact, you wouldn't have been able to see it after it fell, either!

Japanese: コナゴナに砕けた破片を見ても、 それがスタンドだとはわからない！
English: There's no way you could have recognized the broken shards as a glass light stand!

Japanese: ‥‥となると、あなたはいつ、 スタンドを見ることができたか！
English: So, when did you see the stand, Mr. White?

Japanese: それは、倒れた瞬間しかない！
English: It must have been the moment that it fell!

Japanese: そして、それが見えるのは、 綾里法律事務所しかない！
English: And the only place you could have seen that from... is inside the Fey Law Offices!

Japanese: あなたは、犯行があった瞬間 殺人現場にいたんです！
English: In other words, you were at the scene of the crime, when the murder took place!

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおおおお！
English: Gaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa!!

Japanese: こ‥‥小中さん‥‥？
English: Mr... White?

Japanese: 小中さん。
English: Mr. White.

Japanese: あなたが‥‥やったんですね？
English: You did it, didn't you?

Japanese: ミ‥‥ミスタ・サイバンチョ‥‥
English: Mr. Your Honor.

Japanese: ぼかぁ‥‥。 ぼかぁ、ミス・チヒロを‥‥ I... I...
English: Miss Mia...

Japanese: （ふう‥‥どうやら、 勝負は決まったな‥‥）
English: (Heh. Looks like we're about to get our verdict!)

Japanese: そこまでだ‥‥成歩堂　龍一ッ！
English: That's far enough, Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: な、なにッ！ （くそっ！　コイツを忘れてた！）
English: W-what!? (Urk! I forgot about Edgeworth!)

Japanese: 小中氏‥‥。
English: Mr. White...

Japanese: もうそろそろ‥‥
English: I think the time has come.

Japanese: いさぎよく、罪を認めてしまったら いかがなものだろうか？
English: Shouldn't you confess your crime now, hmm?

Japanese: フ‥‥フワッツ？
English: W... what?

Japanese: あなたが犯した犯罪を、 認めるのだよ‥‥。
English: I said, you should confess your crime.

Japanese: すなわち、
English: Ergo,

Japanese: “盗聴”のことを、ね。
English: confess that you placed the wiretap!

Japanese: と‥‥盗聴だって！
English: The w-wiretap!?

Japanese: せ、静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: み、御剣検事！ 今の発言は、どういう‥‥！
English: Mr. Edgeworth! Explain to the court what you mean by this!

Japanese: みなさん。
English: Distinguished members of the court...

Japanese: 小中氏は今、少しコンランして おられる。
English: Mr. White is slightly confused.

Japanese: 私の方から説明しよう。
English: Allow me to explain.

Japanese: （なんか‥‥雲行きが アヤしくなってきたぞ‥‥）
English: (I really don't like the way this is headed...)

Japanese: ごぞんじのとおり、小中氏は コナカルチャーの社長だ。
English: As you know, Mr. White is the CEO of Bluecorp.

Japanese: 彼は、秘書の松竹　梅世に 命令して、盗聴をさせていたのだ。
English: He ordered his secretary, Miss April May, to tap the law offices of Ms. Fey.

Japanese: そ、それがどうかしましたか？
English: What does that have to do...?

Japanese: ‥‥問題は。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: “誰が”“いつ”盗聴器を 綾里法律事務所に仕掛けたか、だ。
English: The question is: when was the wiretap placed in the office, and by who?

Japanese: ま、まさか！
English: No! You wouldn't!

Japanese: ‥‥小中氏。
English: Mr. White.

Japanese: 盗聴器を仕掛けるため、 あなたが綾里の事務所に侵入した。
English: In order to place the wiretap, you entered Ms. Fey's office.

Japanese: ‥‥間違いないだろうか？
English: Am I correct...?

Japanese: イ、イエス！　それだ！ そのとおりだミスタ・ケンジ！
English: C-correct! You are most correct, Miles!

Japanese: な、なんだって！
English: Give me a break!

Japanese: ぼかぁ‥‥盗聴器を仕掛けるため、 綾里法律事務所に行ったんだ！
English: Yes... in order to place the wiretap, I breached the Fey & Co. Law Offices!

Japanese: そのときに、あの電気スタンドを 見たんだよ！
English: That is when I saw that accursed light stand!

Japanese: つまり、どういうことですか、 御剣検事！
English: Now I'm confused. Please explain to the court what all this means, Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: よろしいか。
English: Gladly, Your Honor.

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一の主張は、こうだ。
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright has made his position quite clear.

Japanese: “小中氏は、殺人現場にガラスの スタンドがあるのを知っていた”
English: He has determined that Mr. White knew the glass stand was in the office.

Japanese: “しかし、彼がそのスタンドを 見ることができたのは”
English: He has shown that there was only one time Mr. White could have seen the stand:

Japanese: “殺人事件が起こった瞬間しか なかった”
English: At the very moment of the murder!

Japanese: “だから、小中氏が犯人である”
English: Thus, Mr. Wright would like you to believe that Mr. White was the murderer!

Japanese: そうですね‥‥。
English: I see.

Japanese: しかし。
English: However!

Japanese: 事件が起こるずっと以前に、 小中氏は現場に行ったのだ。
English: It is a fact that Mr. White had been to that office well before the murder took place!

Japanese: 盗聴器を仕掛けるために。
English: He went to place the wiretap!

Japanese: そのときにガラスのスタンドを 見ていれば、
English: He could have seen the glass light stand then.

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一のスイリは、 完全にくずれてしまうのだ！
English: Ergo, Mr. Phoenix Wright's theory is revealed for the baseless conjecture it is!

Japanese: 小中さん！
English: Mr. White!

Japanese: そのときのことを《証言》 してください！
English: You will testify to the court about this wiretap!

Japanese: アハン。 ‥‥まかせてくれたまえ！
English: Ahem. Leave it to me!

Japanese: （目‥‥目まいがしてきた‥‥）
English: (I... I feel faint.)

Japanese: ～盗聴器を仕掛けたとき～
English: -- The Wiretapping --

Japanese: あれはそう、９月のアタマだから、 事件の約１週間、前かな。
English: It was the beginning of September... the week before the murder.

Japanese: ぼかぁ、綾里法律事務所に侵入 したんだよ。
English: I had entered the Fey & Co. Law Offices.

Japanese: モチロン、 盗聴器を仕掛けるためだよ。
English: Of course, I had done so to place the wiretap.

Japanese: そのときにね。たしかに見たんだ。 ガラス製の電気スタンドを‥‥。
English: That is when I saw this glass light stand.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 事件の夜、物音を聞いただけで スタンドだとわかったのは、
English: So, you saw the stand before the night of the incident...

Japanese: 前もって、それを見ていたから ですか。
English: And this is how you were able to identify what had fallen over. By the sound?

Japanese: イエス・アイ・ドゥー！ そのとおりだよ。
English: Correct! That is right.

Japanese: なるほど‥‥。 では、成歩堂くん。《尋問》を。
English: I see. Very well, Mr. Wright, you may cross-examine.

Japanese: （くそ‥‥！ どうやって攻めればいいんだ！）
English: (Gah! What am I supposed to do now...!?)

Japanese: 弁護士さん‥‥。
English: Good luck, Phoenix...

Japanese: ～盗聴器を仕掛けたとき～
English: -- The Wiretapping --

Japanese: あれはそう、９月のアタマだから、 事件の約１週間、前かな。
English: It was the beginning of September... the week before the murder.

Japanese: ぼかぁ、綾里法律事務所に侵入 したんだよ。
English: I had entered the Fey & Co. Law Offices.

Japanese: モチロン、 盗聴器を仕掛けるためだよ。
English: Of course, I had done so to place the wiretap.

Japanese: そのときにね。たしかに見たんだ。 ガラス製の電気スタンドを‥‥。
English: That is when I saw this glass light stand.

Japanese: （くそ‥‥攻めようがないぞ‥‥）
English: (Dammit! There's nothing there for me to press him on!)

Japanese: （とにかく、ゆさぶりまくって 突破口を見つけるしかないな）
English: (Oh well... maybe he's rattled enough that I can bluff something out of him!)

Japanese: そ、その証拠はあるんですか！
English: D-do you have proof!?

Japanese: 松竹　梅世は、電話の会話内容を 知っていた！
English: Miss April May knew the details of Ms. Fey's phone conversation!

Japanese: それは、盗聴器が事件の起こる 以前に仕掛けられていた証拠だ！
English: This proves that the wiretap was placed before the murder!
Huh... right.

Japanese: 本当に、あなたが 侵入したんですか？
English: Was it really you that went into the office?

Japanese: あるいは、梅世さんが‥‥
English: Or was it Miss May?

Japanese: 綾里法律事務所に、正体不明の 古い指紋があった。
English: Unidentified fingerprints several days old were found in the Fey & Co. Law Offices.

Japanese: おそらく、小中氏の指紋だろう。
English: Those were obviously Mr. White's.

Japanese: （御剣め‥‥きっと、もう 指紋は調べているに違いないな）
English: (And if I know Edgeworth, he's already run a check on those prints...)

Japanese: さて、証人。‥‥なんのために、 綾里法律事務所へ‥‥？
English: Now, Mr. White. Tell us why you went to the Fey & Co. Law Offices.

Japanese: なぜ、千尋さんの電話に 盗聴器を仕掛けたんですか！
English: Why did you tap Mia's phone!?

Japanese: それは、本件とは関係のない ことだよ‥‥
English: This has no bearing on the current case, Your Honor.

Japanese: コナカルチャーは いちおう、“探偵業”でね。
English: Bluecorp is a detective agency of sorts.

Japanese: 依頼人に対する 守秘義務があるんだよ。
English: We have a responsibility to protect client confidentiality!

Japanese: なぜ、電気スタンドなんかを 目にとめたんですか！
English: Why did you notice something as innocuous as a light stand!?

Japanese: あのスタンド、すべてのパーツが ガラス製でね。
English: The light stand was made entirely out of glass.

Japanese: あんまりキレイだったんで、 印象に残ったんだ‥‥。
English: It was quite stylish, so I guess it made a lasting impression on me.

Japanese: ハウ・ビューティホー・ザ・ デンキスタンド・イトワズ！
English: Such a beautacious thing deserves attention, does it not?

Japanese: ‥‥ってところかな。
English: That is all.

Japanese: （だめだ！　もう、 攻撃しようがない！）
English: (Uh oh! Don't tell me I've run out of ammo!)

Japanese: クックックッ‥‥。 そこまでかな、弁護士クン。
English: Tsk tsk. I'm afraid that's as far as you go, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: では、そろそろ あきらめてもらおうか。
English: The time has come for you to admit your defeat!

Japanese: おつかれさま‥‥。
English: You fought... honorably.
(No more...)
(I can't take this anymore...)

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright?

Japanese: もう、あきらめますか？
English: Are you giving up?
Y-yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: （なるほどくん）
English: (Phoenix!)

Japanese: （なるほどくん、こっちよ！）
English: (Phoenix! Over here!)

Japanese: （この声は‥‥）
English: (I know that voice!)

Japanese: （ち、千尋さん！）
English: (M-Mia!?)

Japanese: あきらめちゃダメよ、 なるほどくん！
English: Never give up, Phoenix!
M-M-Mia...!
Where... Where am I?

Japanese: ‥‥休憩室、か‥‥。
English: The waiting lobby...?

Japanese: ぼくは、どうして‥‥？
English: What happened?

Japanese: そうだ‥‥。
English: Oh, right...

Japanese: 裁判に負けて、
English: I lost the trial.

Japanese: マボロシを‥‥見たんだっけ。
English: I was... hallucinating.

Japanese: ‥‥気がついたみたいね。
English: Ah, you're finally awake.

Japanese: ギャッ！
English: Gak!

Japanese: あ、ちょ、ちょっと！ なるほどくん！
English: H-hey! Phoenix!

Japanese: シツレイね‥‥。 人のカオを見て“ギャッ”なんて。
English: Gak? That's no way to greet an old friend!

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。 よく見て、私を‥‥。
English: Phoenix, I want you to look at me.

Japanese: き‥‥きみは‥‥
English: Y-You're...

Japanese: ま、真宵‥‥ちゃん？
English: M-Maya...?

Japanese: 綾里家の女は、霊力が強いの。 ‥‥知らなかったかしら？
English: Didn't you know the Fey women have strong psychic powers...?

Japanese: あなたが“負け”を認めようとした 最後の瞬間‥‥、
English: When you accepted your defeat in court...

Japanese: 真宵の“チカラ”が、やっと 目覚めたみたい。
English: It appears that was enough of a shock to awaken Maya's true powers.

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃんが、 霊媒‥‥してるんだよね‥‥？
English: So... Maya is channeling you, Mia?

Japanese: そう、私は真宵‥‥でも、 今は千尋なの。
English: That's right. I am Maya, but I'm also Mia.

Japanese: いい？　なるほどくん。
English: Now, I want you to listen to me, Phoenix.

Japanese: 真宵はあきらめなかったわ。 だから、私は今、ここにいる。
English: Maya never gave up. You can't either!

Japanese: あなたも、あきらめちゃダメ！
English: That's what I came here to tell you.

Japanese: で、でも‥‥！
English: B-but!

Japanese: 時間がないわ。 ‥‥聞きなさい。
English: We don't have much time, Phoenix. Now listen.

Japanese: 実はね‥‥‥。 あなたは、もう勝っているの。
English: You've already won.
Huh...?

Japanese: あなたが持っている証拠品の中に、 《領収書》があるわね？
English: You have that Receipt in the court record, right?

Japanese: あ、ああ。‥‥千尋さんが “マヨイ”って書いた‥‥
English: Um... oh, yeah! The one you wrote "Maya" on...?

Japanese: バカね。それは小中が書いたに 決まってるでしょ。
English: Phoenix! White wrote that, not me!

Japanese: ‥‥で、 それがどうかしたんですか？
English: So... so what do I do with it?

Japanese: その証拠品、ウラ面を 見てみなさい。
English: Look at the front of the receipt!

Japanese: ウラ‥‥ですか？
English: The... front?
(It's a regular receipt...)

Japanese: （発行したのは、有名なデパート みたいだ）
English: (Looks like it's from a famous department store.)
spender!)

Japanese: （“品名‥‥ ガラス製の電気スタンド”！）
English: ("Item"... glass light stand...!)
("Date of purchase"... September 4...)
September 4!

Japanese: ‥‥そうよ、なるほどくん。
English: That's right, Phoenix.

Japanese: 私がそのスタンドを買ったのは あの事件の前日のことなの。
English: I bought that stand the day before I was killed!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: Whoa!

Japanese: さっき小中は、 なんて証言していたかしら？
English: Now, what did Mr. White say in his testimony?

Japanese: あれはそう、９月のアタマだから、 事件の約１週間前、かな。
English: It was the beginning of September... the week before the murder.

Japanese: ‥‥スタンドを見たのは、 事件の１週間前、って‥‥。
English: He said he saw the stand the week before the murder!

Japanese: ‥‥さあ！
English: There you go!

Japanese: そろそろ審理が再開される時間よ！
English: I think the court is about to reconvene!

Japanese: 行くわよ、なるほどくん。 ‥‥“無罪”を勝ちとるためにね！
English: Go do it, Phoenix! You know you're innocent, now you just have to prove it!

Japanese: は‥‥はい！
English: Right!

Japanese: 証拠品《領収書》のデータを 書きなおした。
English: Receipt updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: ９月９日　午後１時１６分 地方裁判所　第１法廷
English: September 9, 1:16 PM District Court Courtroom No. 1

Japanese: これより、成歩堂　龍一の 法廷を再開します。
English: The court will now reconvene for the trial of Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥だいじょうぶかな？ 成歩堂くん。
English: Is the defendant... rather, are you alright, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: はい。 ‥‥すみませんでした。
English: Yes. Sorry, Your Honor.
I'm fine now.

Japanese: ‥‥では、先ほどのつづきに もどりましょうか。
English: Then, let's start where we left off.

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: 我々に、もどるべき “つづき”など、存在しない！
English: There is nothing to go back to!

Japanese: すでに、小中氏の尋問は 終わっている！
English: The cross-examination of Mr. White is finished!

Japanese: あとは、被告人・成歩堂　龍一に 判決を言い渡すだけだ‥‥。
English: All that is required now is for you to pass judgment on the defendant Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: もう一度だけ、 チャンスをください。
English: Please, give me one more chance.

Japanese: ‥‥これで、最後です！
English: I promise you, this is the last time I'll ask you!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: しかし、もう勝負は ついてるのは、たしかですね。
English: But, as Mr. Edgeworth has noted, the trial is more or less finished.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣検事。 どうしますか？
English: Mr. Edgeworth, do you have an opinion on this matter?

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一‥‥。 最後のチャンスをくれてやろう。
English: I say... let us give Mr. Phoenix Wright his last chance.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well!

Japanese: では、小中氏の《尋問》を おねがいします！
English: You may begin your cross-examination.

Japanese: ～盗聴器を仕掛けたとき～
English: -- The Wiretapping --

Japanese: あれはそう、９月のアタマだから、 事件の約１週間、前かな。
English: It was the beginning of September... the week before the murder.

Japanese: ぼかぁ、綾里法律事務所に侵入 したんだよ。
English: I had entered the Fey & Co. Law Offices.

Japanese: モチロン、 盗聴器を仕掛けるためだよ。
English: Of course, I had done so to place the wiretap.

Japanese: そのときにね。たしかに見たんだ。 ガラス製の、電気スタンドを‥‥。
English: That is when I saw this glass light stand.

Japanese: （‥‥千尋さんがくれた 証拠品をつきつけないと！）
English: (Now I've just got to show him the proof Mia gave me!)

Japanese: その証拠はあるんですか！
English: Do you have proof!?

Japanese: 松竹　梅世は、電話の会話内容を 知っていた。
English: Miss April May knew the details of Ms. Fey's phone conversation!

Japanese: それは、盗聴器が事件の起こる 以前に仕掛けられていた証拠だ！
English: This proves that the wiretap was placed before the murder!
Huh... right.

Japanese: （ダメだ！　千尋さんがくれた 証拠品をつきつけないと！）
English: (Gah! I have to show him the evidence Mia gave me!)

Japanese: 本当に、あなたが 侵入したんですか？
English: Was it really you that went into the office?

Japanese: あるいは、梅世さんが‥‥
English: Or was it Miss May?

Japanese: 綾里法律事務所に、正体不明の 古い指紋があった。
English: Unidentified fingerprints several days old were found in the Fey & Co. Law Offices.

Japanese: さっき、小中氏のものと一致した という報告が入ったよ。
English: Those were obviously Mr. White's.

Japanese: （ダメだ！　千尋さんがくれた 証拠品をつきつけないと！）
English: (Gah! I have to show him the evidence Mia gave me!)

Japanese: さて、証人。‥‥なんのために、 綾里法律事務所へ‥‥？
English: Now, Mr. White. Tell us why you went to the Fey & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: なぜ、千尋さんの電話に 盗聴器を仕掛けたんですか！
English: Why did you tap Mia's phone!?

Japanese: それは、本件とは関係のない ことだよ‥‥
English: This has no bearing on the current case, Your Honor.

Japanese: 異議を認めます。 ‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: Objection sustained. Mr. Wright...

Japanese: さっきと同じ質問をしていても、 意味がないと思いますがね‥‥。
English: I don't see how asking the same questions again will make any difference.

Japanese: （ダメだ！　千尋さんがくれた 証拠品をつきつけないと！）
English: (Right! I have to show him the evidence Mia gave me!)

Japanese: なぜ、電気スタンドなんかを 目にとめたんですか！
English: Why did you notice something as innocuous as a light stand!?

Japanese: あのスタンド、すべてのパーツが ガラス製でね。
English: The light stand was made entirely out of glass.

Japanese: あまりにキレイだったんで、 印象に残ったんだ‥‥。
English: It was quite stylish, so I guess it made a lasting impression on me.

Japanese: これを見てください。
English: Look closely at this.

Japanese: 血文字で“マヨイ”‥‥。
English: See the word "Maya" written in blood...?

Japanese: ハッ！ 今となっては、意味のない‥‥
English: Bwah hah! You're grasping!

Japanese: 問題は、これが領収書のウラ である、ということです。
English: I think not. Look at the other side of the receipt.

Japanese: な、なんだと？
English: Th-the other side?

Japanese: 裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: その“領収書”には、 何が書いてありますか？
English: Would you tell the court what is written on the other side of that receipt?

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...
Well!

Japanese: “ガラス製の電気スタンド‥‥”
English: A "glass light stand"!

Japanese: 発行日は‥‥
English: And the date of purchase...

Japanese: 事件の前日です！
English: Why, that's the day before the murder!

Japanese: ‥‥つまり！
English: You see!

Japanese: 小中さん。
English: Mr. White.

Japanese: あなたが綾里法律事務所に 侵入した９月のアタマには‥‥
English: When you allegedly entered Fey & Co. Law Offices at the beginning of September...

Japanese: あのスタンドは、 まだなかったんですよ！
English: The stand could not have been there!

Japanese: ホ、ホワァァァァァァァァァァァァ ァァァァァァァァァァァァァァァァ
English: Kwaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaaa

Japanese: ァァァァァァァァァァァァァァァァ ァァァァァァァァァァァァァット！
English: aaaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaahh!?

Japanese: どうですか？　小中さん。
English: Well, Mr. White?

Japanese: まだ、言い逃れ、できますか？
English: Can't get out of this one, can you?

Japanese: ノー・アイ・キャント‥‥。
English: No... It's impossachievable...

Japanese: （おやおや、 放心しちまったよ‥‥）
English: (Uh oh... he's losing it.)

Japanese: さあ。‥‥裁判長。
English: Well, Your Honor.

Japanese: あなた方に、いくら強い圧力が かかっていても、
English: I understand there must be quite a bit of PRESSURE on you.

Japanese: こうなった以上、ぼくを有罪にする ことは、できないはずです。
English: But I think you'll agree you can't judge me "guilty" under these circumstances.

Japanese: わかりました‥‥。
English: Very well.

Japanese: それでは、審理はこれで終了‥‥
English: Then, that is all for the trial of...

Japanese: そこまでだ‥‥成歩堂　龍一ッ！
English: Not so fast, Phoenix Wright!
Eh?

Japanese: （おいおい、ウソだろ‥‥？）
English: (What! No way can he worm his way out of this one!)

Japanese: （まだ食い下がるつもりかよ！）
English: (Oh wait... I forgot. It's Edgeworth.)

Japanese: 弁護人の意見は、たしかに 一理ある‥‥。
English: There is a certain thread of logic to the defendant's claims.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし！
English: However!

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一が無実であるという 決定的な証拠も、ないのだ！
English: There is no concrete proof that Phoenix Wright is innocent!

Japanese: そこで。
English: Ergo!

Japanese: ‥‥あと１日、 判決を待ってもらいたい。
English: I would like to request one more day before Phoenix Wright is granted his freedom.

Japanese: もう一度、 調査をしなおす時間がほしい。
English: I need time to make one more inquiry into this matter.

Japanese: ほ、ほう‥‥。
English: Hmm...!

Japanese: （も、もう一度、調査だと‥‥？）
English: (Another inquiry...!?)

Japanese: （まさか‥‥千尋さんの 解剖記録みたいに、）
English: (This isn't going to be another one of those updated autopsy reports!)

Japanese: （また、何か証拠を デッチ上げる気じゃあ‥‥！）
English: (This guy just makes up evidence as he pleases!)
(This is bad...!)

Japanese: 小中の有罪は、明白です！
English: Mr. White's guilt is obvious!

Japanese: これ以上、審理をのばす 必要は、まったくありません！
English: There is no need to prolong this trial any further!

Japanese: ふむう。
English: Hmm.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣検事、いかがかな？
English: Well, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: しかし、小中氏を起訴するのは、 私たち検察側だ。
English: If anyone is going to call Mr. White to trial, it would be me, the prosecution.

Japanese: キミの推理が正しいかどうか、 １日、調査したい。
English: I need a day to ascertain whether your claims have any basis in factual evidence!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: I see.

Japanese: 成歩堂くんの異議を却下します！
English: Objection denied!

Japanese: えええええ！
English: Whaaaat!

Japanese: （とりあえず、ようすを 見てみるか‥‥）
English: (Hmm. I don't know where he's going with this. I'd better hold back for now.)

Japanese: そうですね‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: 弁護人の異議もないようですから、 御剣くんの意見を認めましょう。
English: As there is no objection from the defense, I grant Mr. Edgeworth his extra day.

Japanese: （し、しまった！）
English: (Uh oh...)

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一の審理は、 明日まで延期します！
English: The completion of the trial of Mr. Phoenix Wright will be postponed until tomorrow.

Japanese: （ダメだ！　今ここで 決着をつけなかったら！）
English: (No! There's no telling what will happen if I can't end this now!)

Japanese: （御剣はきっと“何か” やるに違いない！）
English: (Edgeworth is sure to come up with--or just make up-- something!)

Japanese: （‥‥せっかく、千尋さんに 助けてもらったのに‥‥）
English: (And after Mia showed up to help me and all...)

Japanese: ミスタ・サイバンチョ！
English: Mr. Your Honor!

Japanese: ぼかぁ、もう 帰っていいのかな‥‥？
English: May I go home?

Japanese: もちろんかまいません。 ‥‥ごくろうさまでした。
English: Of course. Thank you for your time.
(Gah!)

Japanese: 証人、待ちなさい！
English: The witness will stay!

Japanese: （ち、千尋さん‥‥！）
なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix!

Japanese: このメモを読み上げなさい。
English: Read this note out loud.

Japanese: 千尋さん、これは‥‥？
English: Mia? What's this...?

Japanese: 千尋さんからメモを 受け取った。
English: Memo received from Mia.

Japanese: 裁判長。 ‥‥ちょっとよろしいですか。
English: Your Honor. If I may...?

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。‥‥あなたもなかなか シツコイですねえ‥‥。
English: You're quite persistent today, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: （あたりまえだ！　こっちは、 命がかかってるんだぞ！）
English: (You bet I am! My life is riding on this one!)

Japanese: これからぼくが読み上げるものを 聞いていただきたい！
English: I have something I would like to read to the court!

Japanese: （いや、違う！　‥‥ここで 間違ったら、おしまいだぞ！）
English: (No... that's not it! I can't mess this one up!)

Japanese: どうかしましたか？
English: Something the matter?

Japanese: い、いえ！
English: N-no, Your Honor!

Japanese: ぼくは、千尋さんがくれた リストを読み上げた。
English: The memo Mia had given me was a list of names.

Japanese: ‥‥どこかで聞いたことのある 名前が、ならんでいる。
English: Many of them sounded strangely familiar.

Japanese: これは‥‥政財界の 有名人たちの名前、か‥‥。
English: People in finance, famous celebrities...

Japanese: そのとき！
English: That's when it happened.

Japanese: スタァァァァァァァァップ！
English: S-s-stop! Desist! Halt!

Japanese: や、や、やめろ！ やめさせろォッ！
English: P-please, stop! Make him stop!

Japanese: ‥‥な、なんでキサマが‥‥ そのリストを持ってるんだ‥‥？
English: How... how did you get that list!?

Japanese: ‥‥証人。
English: Mr. White.

Japanese: あなたがここで 罪を認めないのであれば、
English: Admit your guilt, right here, right now.

Japanese: このリストは、 マスコミに公表されるでしょう。
English: Or else this list will be released to the press!

Japanese: ‥‥認める‥‥
English: I... I confess.

Japanese: 認めるよ‥‥
English: I confess.

Japanese: ぼかぁ‥‥ぼかぁ‥‥ ‥‥ミス・チヒロを‥‥
English: I... I did it. I hit her.

Japanese: あの‥‥《考える人》で‥‥ ‥‥殴ったんだ！
English: I hit Miss Mia with The Thinker!

Japanese: ‥‥以上です、裁判長。
English: Case closed, Your Honor.

Japanese: どうやら、これ以上の審理は 必要ないようですね。
English: Well, I see no reason to continue this trial.

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: また、やってくれましたね‥‥。
English: You've done it again! That was quite a spirited defense!

Japanese: いやあ‥‥。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: たまたまツイていただけですよ。 （スゴ腕の霊が‥‥）
English: I guess you could say that. (If only you knew how spirit-ed it was!)

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥では！
English: Hmm. Well!

Japanese: 弁護人‥‥いや、被告人 成歩堂　龍一に、
English: This court finds the defense...

Japanese: 判決を言い渡します。
English: Ahem! Rather, the defendant, Mr. Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: ‥‥では、 本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: ９月９日　午後２時２４分 地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: September 9, 2:24 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1

Japanese: またこうして“おめでとう”って 言えるなんて。
English: Well, I never thought I'd be saying this again...

Japanese: 綾里家に生まれたことに、 カンシャしないとね。
English: But, congratulations! You're lucky I was born a Fey!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんにも。
English: I'm lucky I had both you and Maya on my side.

Japanese: ‥‥そうね。
English: I'm glad you made it.

Japanese: ありがとう、なるほどくん。
English: Thank you, Phoenix.

Japanese: 私と真宵のために‥‥ こんな、あぶない目にあって‥‥。
English: You risked a lot to help me... and Maya.

Japanese: 私、一生わすれません。
English: I won't forget it as long as I live!
(As long as you "live"...?)

Japanese: ‥‥そろそろ、時間ね。
English: My time here's running out.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: 真宵の霊力は、まだとても 弱いわ‥‥。
English: Maya's powers are still weak.

Japanese: そんなに長く、“こっち”には いられないの。
English: I can't stay here that long.

Japanese: そ、そんな！ ‥‥まだ、言いたいことが‥‥！
English: W-what!? No! There's still so much to say!

Japanese: 心配しないで。
English: Don't worry.

Japanese: ‥‥また会えるわ。きっと。
English: I'm sure we'll meet again.

Japanese: しょ、所長！
English: Ch-Chief!

Japanese: ‥‥ふふ。 もう私は“所長”じゃないわ。
English: Hee hee. I'm not the "Chief" anymore.

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix?

Japanese: 今夜９時。‥‥事務所に 来てくれるかしら‥‥？
English: Can you come to the office tonight... say, 9:00?

Japanese: 事務所に‥‥。
English: The office...?

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥また会いましょうね。
English: I'll see you later.

Japanese: 所長‥‥千尋さん！
English: Chief... Mia!

Japanese: ９月９日　午後９時２分 綾里法律事務所
English: September 9, 9:02 PM Fey & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: （‥‥あの夜のこと、 思い出しちまうな‥‥）
English: (Being here, it's hard not to think about that night...)

Japanese: よく来てくれたわね‥‥。
English: You came!

Japanese: 千尋さん‥‥。
English: Mia...

Japanese: 来てくれないんじゃないかと、 少し心配だったわ。
English: I was kinda worried you might not.

Japanese: そんなわけ、ないじゃないですか。
English: Huh? Of course I came...

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ。
English: Well then!

Japanese: おなかもすいたことだし、 みそラーメン食べに行きましょう。
English: I'm pretty hungry. How about a burger?

Japanese: ち、千尋さん‥‥？
English: M-Mia...?

Japanese: あっはっはっは！ おもしろいカオー。
English: Bwah hah hah! You should see your face!

Japanese: 千尋さんッ！
English: Mia!

Japanese: さっきから“千尋さん”ばっかり。 ‥‥あたしですよー、真宵。
English: What are you talking about? It's me! Maya!

Japanese: ま、真宵ちゃん？
English: M-Maya...?

Japanese: ね、ね、似てました？　あたし？
English: What, did I look like my sister?

Japanese: （“似てた”、っていうか、 本人じゃないか‥‥）
English: (Look like... you WERE her!)

Japanese: ふーん。これは使えそうだな‥‥。
English: Hmm! I might be able to use that.

Japanese: “なるほどくん、私、お茶が 飲みたいわ”とか言っちゃって。
English: "Oh, Phoenix! Go to the store and buy me lunch, would you?"

Japanese: ‥‥あのさ。 なんできみが、ここに？
English: Uhm, Maya... Why are you here?

Japanese: ほら！　‥‥これ。
English: Because of this!

Japanese: お姉ちゃんが、手紙くれたんです！
English: See? Mia wrote me a letter.

Japanese: “なるほどくんのこと、 よろしくたのむわ”‥‥って。
English: Take care of Phoenix for me.

Japanese: ‥‥何をよろしくたのむの？
English: Take care of... huh?

Japanese: 事務所ですよ、事・務・所。
English: She means the office! This office!

Japanese: 《成歩堂法律事務所》。 あたし、お手伝いしますから！
English: Someone has to help with the new Wright & Co. Law Offices, right?

Japanese: よろしくおねがいします！
English: And who better but me! Maya Fey, reporting for duty!

Japanese: ‥‥あ、カタ苦しいのは ナシにしましょうね！
English: Wait, no, on second thought, let's make this casual!

Japanese: よろしく！　なるほどくん！
English: Yo, Nick! Maya here, ready to get down to business!

Japanese: ‥‥あ、お名前はお姉ちゃんから 聞いたんだよ。
English: You... don't mind me calling you "Nick," do you?

Japanese: いい名前だねー、 “なるほどくん”なんて。
English: It's a great name! Mia said that's what your friend Larry calls you.

Japanese: （な、“なるほどくん”って‥‥）
ね。ほら、 あくしゅ、あくしゅ。
English: You know what this means? We're partners!

Japanese: ‥‥でもまあ、考えてみれば、
English: You know, when I think about it...

Japanese: ぼくがこうしてここに 立っているのも、
English: It is Maya's fault I'm here now.

Japanese: もとはと言えば、この子の せいであり、
English: But...

Japanese: 同時に、この子のおかげでもある。
English: If it wasn't for her I'd probably be in jail!

Japanese: 《成歩堂法律事務所》‥‥ ヒビキは悪くない、な。
English: Wright & Co. Law Offices. It's got a good ring to it.

Japanese: ‥‥うん。
English: Yeah!

Japanese: よろしくたのむよ、真宵ちゃん。
English: Thanks, Maya!

Japanese: （‥‥なるほどくん。 しっかりね‥‥）
English: (Good luck, Phoenix.)

Japanese: （私はいつでも、 あなたを見てるわ‥‥）
English: (I'll always be here... watching.)

Japanese: じゃ！　なるほどくん。 行こう！
English: Right! Okay, Nick, let's do it!

Japanese: え、え‥‥？　どこに。
English: Huh? Do... what?

Japanese: 決まってるでしょ。 みそラーメン。
English: Burgers, dummy! Burgers!

Japanese: 近くに、おいしいところが あるんだよ。
English: There's a great burger joint just down the street.

Japanese: ‥‥ほら。早く！
English: C'mon! Time's a wasting!

Japanese: はいはい‥‥。
English: O-okay! Wait up!

Japanese: おわり
English: THE END

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: 弁護人の反証は、検察側の主張を くつがえす力を持っていない！
English: The defense has failed to give the court sufficient reason to doubt the prosecution's claim.

Japanese: 弁護‥‥被告人、成歩堂　龍一に この場で判決を言い渡します！
English: This court finds the defense, er, rather, the defendant, Mr. Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより、その身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!
